$comment: Machine-readable data-source licence registry. Every ETL module must declare a SOURCE_ID that exists here with ingestion=allowed. CI enforces this (scripts/check-registry.mjs). Statuses: allowed | pending (awaiting licence clarification — do not ingest) | prohibited (never ingest).

sources.name: Canal & River Trust stoppage/navigation notices API
sources.notes: All five query params (consult, geometry, start, end, fields) are required or the API returns HTTP 500. geometry=line is broken server-side. No CORS — native/proxy only. Poll politely with a descriptive User-Agent. Breakage-prone: validate defensively, alert on schema drift. Outreach to CRT for a blessing is planned.

sources.name: CRT open data — OGL v3 datasets (locks, bridges, winding holes, wharves, tunnel portals, weirs, slipways, reservoirs, boat lifts, dry docks, aqueducts, culverts)
sources.attribution: Contains Canal & River Trust data © Canal & River Trust, licensed under the Open Government Licence v3.0
sources.cadence: monthly refresh; nightly ETL pull is fine
sources.notes: Licence is PER DATASET — verify each item's licence via DCAT/data.gov.uk before adding it to a pipeline. FeatureServers at services.arcgis.com/DknzyjEEie5tEW0u.

sources.name: CRT legacy 2019 facility/mooring layers (Customer_Service_Facilities, Water_Point, Elsan, Pump_Out, Refuse_Disposal, Mooring_Site, Boatyards — *_View_Public)
sources.licence: CRT data licence (attribution required; non-commercial use only — see docs/licensing.md)
sources.attribution: © The Canal & River Trust copyright and database rights reserved
sources.cadence: static since Dec 2019; weekly pull to detect migration
sources.notes: Only source of facility/mooring point data. Page with resultOffset (transfer limit 1000-2000). The non-commercial clause is a hard constraint: the app must remain ad-free and payment-free. Open/closed status is NOT in these layers — it comes from notices typeId 10 + the community layer.

sources.name: CRT canal centreline / Canals By Navigation / network geometry
sources.licence: CRT data licence (non-commercial) with contradictory licence-of-record (data.gov.uk says other-nc)
sources.notes: DO NOT SHIP. Licence record is contradictory and non-commercial. OSM waterway geometry is essentially complete for UK canals and ODbL-clean — use that instead. Revisit only with written clarification from CRT.

sources.name: OpenStreetMap — Geofabrik Great Britain extract
sources.notes: Primary source for all waterway geometry, locks, bridges, pubs, shops. Keep OSM-derived tables separately provenanced (ODbL share-alike). Never use public Overpass in production paths — osmium over this extract instead.

sources.name: Environment Agency real-time flood-monitoring API (levels, flows, warnings, tide gauges)
sources.attribution: this uses Environment Agency flood and river level data from the real-time data API (Beta)
sources.cadence: 15 min upstream; poll hourly and cache
sources.notes: No key. CORS *. Use cache-friendly query patterns per EA guidance. Exact attribution string above is required verbatim.

sources.name: River Thames current conditions & restrictions (GOV.UK Content API)
sources.attribution: Contains public sector information licensed under the Open Government Licence v3.0
sources.cadence: updated daily ~11:00; poll a few times per day
sources.notes: Reach-by-reach board status is embedded govspeak HTML inside details.body — needs a parser; format may change without notice.

sources.name: Food Standards Agency — Food Hygiene Rating Scheme API + bulk XML
sources.attribution: Contains Food Standards Agency data © Crown copyright
sources.notes: Requires header x-api-version: 2 and a real User-Agent. Scotland uses FHIS (Pass/Improvement Required) not numeric ratings.

sources.name: Open-Meteo forecast API
sources.notes: Non-commercial term is a hard constraint alongside the CRT licence: app stays ad-free and payment-free.

sources.name: Ordnance Survey OpenData (OpenZoomstack, OpenRivers, CodePoint Open)
sources.attribution: Contains OS data © Crown copyright and database right

sources.name: Mapillary street-level imagery API
sources.notes: Display as a separate layer/work — CC-BY-SA imagery does not contaminate the ODbL database. The only source with real towpath-level imagery.

sources.name: Google Street View via Maps URLs deep link
sources.licence: Free deep link, no API key; opens the Google Maps app/site
sources.notes: NEVER embed Street View imagery beside a non-Google map (Google Maps Platform ToS). Deep links only.

sources.name: Scottish Canals data portal shapefiles (centrelines, locks, moorings, towpaths)
sources.notes: No licence stated on the portal. Email gis@scottishcanals.co.uk before ingesting (outreach task).

sources.name: Broads Authority moorings FeatureServer
sources.notes: Rich fields (capacity, cost, water_point, mooring_length) but unlicensed and draft. Centreline IS available under OGL via data.gov.uk. Clarify before ingesting the moorings layer.

sources.name: Ofcom mobile coverage API (per-operator 4G/5G, 50 m grid)
sources.notes: Baseline for signal-at-mooring; our auto-captured community samples are the ground truth either way.
